seo.title: Building Customer Support with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Instructor enables structured extraction of user intents, ticket details, and escalation triggers from unstructured conversations. Instructor patches LLM clients to return validated Pydantic models, but hallucinations, partial extractions, and schema violations can lead to incorrect ticket creation or missed escalations. Vex guardrails ensure response accuracy, PII filtering, and policy compliance in high-risk RAG+CRM environments.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with user queries routed through a RAG pipeline that retrieves relevant knowledge base articles and CRM customer data. Instructor-wrapped LLM calls extract structured outputs like TicketRequest (with fields for issue_type, priority, customer_id) and ResponsePlan (tone, escalation_trigger, suggested_actions). Tool calling handles CRM lookups and ticket creation via APIs.

The agent maintains conversation state, validating each turn against Pydantic schemas for consistency. Nested extractions handle complex cases like multi-issue tickets or sentiment analysis with policy checks. Failures trigger retries or human escalation.

Vex integrates at verification points to catch hallucinations, enforce tone guidelines, filter PII, and ensure compliance before CRM writes or customer responses.

risks.name: Structured output validation retries exhausted
risks.description: Instructor retries fail after max_attempts when LLM consistently produces invalid schemas
risks.mitigation: Vex fallback routing to human agents or simplified schema extraction

risks.name: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
risks.description: LLM generates invalid priority='urgent-asap' when schema expects 'low|medium|high'
risks.mitigation: Vex enum validation with semantic correction to closest valid value

risks.name: Partial extraction missing required fields
risks.description: customer_id or issue_type omitted from TicketRequest despite being in context
risks.mitigation: Vex required field enforcement with RAG context re-injection

risks.name: PII accidentally included in responses
risks.description: Phone numbers, emails, or account numbers leaked in automated replies
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection and redaction before response generation

implementation.title: Basic Ticket Extraction with Instructor
implementation.description: Patch OpenAI client to extract structured TicketRequest from customer query
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from openai import OpenAI
from typing import Literal

class TicketRequest(BaseModel):
    customer_id: str
    issue_type: Literal['billing', 'technical', 'order', 'other']
    priority: Literal['low', 'medium', 'high']
    description: str

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=TicketRequest,
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Extract ticket details from customer message"},
        {"role": "user", "content": "My order #12345 hasn't shipped and I need to update payment"}
    ]
)

ticket = response.model
print(ticket.customer_id)  # '12345'

implementation.title: RAG Context Injection for Accurate Extraction
implementation.description: Combine knowledge base retrieval with customer query for better structured extraction
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List, Literal

class KnowledgeContext(BaseModel):
    articles: List[str]
    customer_history: str

class SupportResponse(BaseModel):
    diagnosis: str
    resolution_steps: List[str]
    escalation_required: bool = False

# Mock RAG retrieval
rag_context = KnowledgeContext(
    articles=["Shipping delays: check carrier status"], 
    customer_history="Repeat billing issues"
)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=SupportResponse,
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Use provided context to diagnose issue"},
        {"role": "user", "content": str(rag_context) + "\nCustomer: Still no shipment!"}
    ]
)

print(response.model.resolution_steps)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Instructor Calls with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex guardrails for PII filtering and policy compliance around Instructor extraction
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel

class SafeTicket(BaseModel):
    customer_id: str
    issue_type: str
    priority: str

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

@verify(guards=[
    "pii_filter",
    "policy_compliance",
    "response_accuracy"
])
def extract_ticket(query: str) -> SafeTicket:
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=SafeTicket,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": query}]
    ).model

# Vex automatically retries failed validations
ticket = extract_ticket("Call me at 555-1234 about order #ABC123")
# PII redacted by Vex guardrail
print(ticket.customer_id)  # 'ABC123' (phone filtered)
